title: athlete — trait composition
description: Two owned traits. `BelongsToAthlete` is the FK trait for downstream models (AthleteGuardian, AthleteEnrollment, TeamMember polymorphic, EventRegistration, SessionAttendance, Membership); `HasAthleteHealthProfile` is composed onto the Athlete model itself + surfaces the medical + emergency-contact accessors with the redactor pipeline applied. Order matters when composed with `BelongsToTenant` / `BelongsToBranch` / `BelongsToUser` — tenant MUST come first so scope chains observe the tenant context; branch and user precede the module-owned trait.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries an `athlete_id` FK. Adds the `athlete()` belongsTo relation, a `resolveInAthlete(?Athlete)` query scope that filters `WHERE athlete_id = ?` (falling back to a caller-supplied resolver when the arg is omitted), and a `booted()` hook that refuses to save a row whose `athlete_id` points at an Athlete in a different tenant than the row itself (defense-in-depth against cross-tenant FK smuggling). Also refuses when the referenced Athlete is not in a coherent state for the operation — configurable per composing model (e.g. AthleteEnrollment refuses when athlete.status = 'archived'; AthleteGuardian permits any non-archived status).
usedBy: athlete-guardian::AthleteGuardian (Wave 3a — CASCADE FK; guardian rows go with the athlete)
teams::TeamMember (Wave 3+ — polymorphic; when member_type='athlete', member_id references Athlete.id)
composition_notes: Order: `BelongsToTenant` must appear BEFORE `BelongsToAthlete` so the tenant scope resolves first.

`BelongsToBranch` is IMPLIED by the athlete (athlete.branch_id) — do NOT compose `BelongsToBranch` on models that already carry `BelongsToAthlete` (the branch cascades through athlete → branch_id in queries).

The `booted()` fill hook is off by default. Turn on per-model via `bool $fillAthleteOnCreating = true` in the composing model (rarely useful outside CLI paths — athlete context is not carried in request headers).

owned.description: Composed onto the Athlete model itself. Adds accessor methods for the medical + emergency-contact clusters that apply the redactor pipeline based on the current caller's permissions. Also exposes `hasActiveConsentFor(purpose: 'photo_release' | 'medical_disclosure' | 'third_party'): bool` (checks the flag + the recorded_at freshness when athlete_consent_workflow entitlement is on).

framework.reason: Archived athletes soft-delete for the retention window (see retention.json) before hard-purge.

Athlete receives lifecycle notifications (welcome / consent-recorded / age-group-changed / graduated / withdrawn) — addressed to the athlete's user_id when set, else the primary guardian's user via routing.

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3.

user.reason: Athlete carries user_id (nullable) + composes BelongsToUser for the .own scope suffix. `athletes.view.own` narrows to `WHERE user_id = current_user_id`.

audit.reason: Every mutation is compliance-relevant. Medical columns write to a SEPARATE audit table (`athlete_medical_history`) with 7-year retention.

activity.reason: Lifecycle transitions + consent recording + age-group snapshot flips feed the tenant activity feed. Medical changes DO NOT feed activity (private-tier).

athlete.notes: Order matters. BelongsToTenant precedes every scope-observing trait. BelongsToUser must come BEFORE HasAthleteHealthProfile so the User relation is registered before the delegated-accessor mixin queries it. No BelongsToOrganization / BelongsToRegion — athlete cascades both through branch. No BelongsToApplication — athlete cascades through tenant per hierarchy.md §2.
